title: First Push
subtitle: Share your work with the remote monastery

briefing.narrative:
The Monastery of Version Control has expanded. There is now a REMOTE monastery — a mirror of your repository that other monks can access.

The Head Monk hands you a scroll: "A remote repository named 'origin' has been set up. Your task is simple, young monk: create meaningful work, then PUSH it to origin so all may benefit from your wisdom."

The cat stretches lazily. It has already pushed its napping schedule to three remote locations.

"Remember: first you commit locally, then you push. The remote only receives what you explicitly share."
briefing.concept:
Remote repositories are copies of your repo that live elsewhere. 'git push' sends your commits to a remote. Before pushing, you need a remote configured with 'git remote add'.

The workflow: commit locally, then push to share.

'git push origin main' pushes your main branch to the remote named 'origin'.
objectives: Create a file called 'scroll.txt' with any content
Add and commit it with a meaningful message
Push your main branch to origin
Verify with 'git branch -r' that origin/main exists

args.content: Welcome to the Monastery of Version Control

hints.text: Create a file first: echo "wisdom" > scroll.txt
Stage and commit: git add scroll.txt && git commit -m "Add scroll"
Push to remote: git push origin main
